actionUnits.condition: Ignition via neighbor threshold. Target MonoBehaviour (script guid 1729fd01c90a64d5b8037e1eef0d000d, component fileID 2592690533154406537) is configured from scene: isOn=0, timeToOnMin=40, timeToOnMax=60, onNeighborsThreshold=2, maxNeighborDistance=4, downMultiplier=2, totalHitTime=5, maxHitInterval=2, mainEffectGo fileID 1849464941111748095. Precondition required: ensure this Fire has at least 2 neighbors within 4 units in world space (and ideally one lower than y-3 to exercise downMultiplier), and notify this Fire that those neighbors are on so that onNeighbors >= 2. This can be achieved by: (a) calling FindNeighbors(Fire[] allFires, int fireId) on all fires, and (b) having two neighbors change to isOn=true which will internally call NotifyNeighborState(true). Once shouldTurnOn is true, wait up to timeToOn (random in [40,60], downMultiplier doubles accumulation if a lower neighbor is present) so that Fire becomes isOn and UpdateFireState(true) executes, playing ParticleSystem and calling FireController.Instance.FireChanged(+1) and NotifyAllNeighbors().

Extinguish via sustained water hits. Precondition: ensure Fire is currently isOn=true (e.g., after previous ignition step). During this trigger window, simulate Water hits by invoking WaterHit(Vector3 normal) on component fileID 2592690533154406537 at intervals less than maxHitInterval (2.0s), e.g., every 0.5s for > totalHitTime (5.0s). Expected: each call sets isTurningOff=true and resets currentHitInterval=0. After cumulative currentHitTime >= 5.0s (within ~6s total), Fire turns off: isOn=false, shouldTurnOn=false, timeToOnDelta=0, isTurningOff=false, currentHitTime=0, UpdateFireState(true) runs (ParticleSystem Stop; FireController.Instance.FireChanged(-1); NotifyAllNeighbors()).

Abort extinguish after interval timeout. Precondition: ensure Fire is isOn=true again (either manually re-ignite by neighbors or set up state before this step). At t=0, invoke one WaterHit(Vector3 normal) on component fileID 2592690533154406537 then do not hit again. Expected: currentHitTime and currentHitInterval start accumulating; when currentHitInterval >= maxHitInterval (2.0s) before totalHitTime is reached, code path resets isTurningOff=false and currentHitTime=0 without changing isOn. This covers the branch where extinguish is canceled due to exceeding maxHitInterval.
